text.title: Domain Audit Workflow and Agent Rules
text.summary: How to run a seven-domain gap audit, map missing artefacts to risks, and constraints for agent output to ensure stable, auditable results.

section.title: Audit Workflow

rule.statement: Walk the seven domains in fixed order (Governance, Scope, Schedule, Finance, Stakeholders, Resources, Risk) and for each emit: artefact_path (or MISSING), owner (or UNASSIGNED), last_updated (ISO date or never), status (green/amber/red), next_action (one imperative sentence).
rule.rationale: Fixed domain order makes diffs across weekly runs stable — status changes are visible as line-level changes in version control without noise from reordering.

rule.statement: Run the domain audit before every steering committee meeting. The output is the status pack, not a separate document that the PM then summarises manually.
rule.rationale: Separate manual summarisation introduces optimism bias and lag. An automated audit from source artefacts is objective and current.

rule.statement: Map each missing domain artefact directly to a risk entry in the Risk domain register. A missing artefact is not a process gap — it is a project risk with a probability and impact.
rule.rationale: Risk registers that do not include process gaps undercount project risk systematically. Governance gaps are often higher-impact than delivery risks.

section.reference: Python domain gap auditor, emits JSON

antipattern.description: LLM output contains "Integration Management" or "Quality Management" as a domain name.
antipattern.reason: These are PMBoK 6 terms; in PMBoK 8 they are integrated. Any output containing these names is anchored to the wrong edition and must be rejected.

antipattern.description: Agent output lists eight domains (adding Quality or Communications as a standalone).
antipattern.reason: Validate output length == 7 domains. An 8th domain signals PMBoK 6 training bias; reject and re-prompt with the canonical seven in the system prompt.

antipattern.description: Domain status colours drift to "green" over time without human sampling.
antipattern.reason: Agents self-validate by confirming artefact existence, not artefact quality. Calibrate by sampling 1 in 5 domains for human review each cycle.
